user_output("Or run from a plan branch with a plan reference file") raise SystemExit(1)
plan_id = ctx.plan_backend.resolve_plan_id_for_branch(ctx.repo.root, branch) if plan_id is None: user_output( click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "No identifier specified and could not infer from branch name" ) user_output("Usage: erk pr view <identifier>") user_output("Or run from a plan branch with a plan reference file") raise SystemExit(1)
identifier = plan_id
plan_number = parse_issue_identifier(identifier) if plan_id is None: plan_id = str(plan_number)
remote = get_remote_github(ctx)
session.assistant: I see - the original human command had extra help lines including "plan reference file". The new code goes through `run_pr_view` which returns `MachineCommandError` and then the human command just prints `result.message`. I need to add those help lines in the human command:
session.tool_result:
245→@click.pass_obj 246→def pr_view( 247→ ctx: ErkContext, 248→ identifier: str | None, 249→ *, 250→ full: bool, 251→ repo_id: GitHubRepoId, 252→) -> None: 253→ """Fetch and display a plan by identifier. 254→ 255→ IDENTIFIER can be a plain number (e.g., "42") or a GitHub issue URL 256→ (e.g., "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123"). 257→ 258→ If not provided, infers the plan number from the current branch name. 259→ 260→ By default, shows only header information. Use --full to display 261→ the complete plan body. 262→ 263→ Examples: 264→ erk pr view 42 265→ erk pr view 42 --full 266→ erk pr view 42 --repo owner/repo 267→ """ 268→ request = PrViewRequest(identifier=identifier, full=full) 269→ result = run_pr_view(ctx, request, repo_id=repo_id)
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

268→ request = PrViewRequest(identifier=identifier, full=full) 269→ result = run_pr_view(ctx, request, repo_id=repo_id) 270→ 271→ if isinstance(result, MachineCommandError): 272→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + result.message) 273→ raise SystemExit(1) 274→ 275→ # For human display we need the Plan object for rich rendering. 276→ # Re-fetch via the same gateway path that run_pr_view used. 277→ plan_number = parse_issue_identifier(result.plan_id) 278→ remote = get_remote_github(ctx) 279→ issue = remote.get_issue(owner=repo_id.owner, repo=repo_id.repo, number=plan_number) 280→ if isinstance(issue, IssueNotFound): 281→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Plan #{result.plan_id} not found") 282→ raise SystemExit(1) 283→ 284→ plan = github_issue_to_plan(issue) 285→ 286→ repo_root = None if isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) else ctx.repo.root 287→ if repo_root is not None: 288→ all_meta = ctx.plan_backend.get_all_metadata_fields(repo_root, result.plan_id) 289→ if isinstance(all_meta, PlanNotFound): 290→ header_info: dict[str, object] = plan.header_fields 291→ else: 292→ header_info = all_meta 293→ else: 294→ header_info = plan.header_fields 295→ 296→ _display_plan(plan, plan_id=result.plan_id, header_info=header_info, full=full) 297→
